feedback.text: Ive been a gamer since I was a kid, and recently it feels like the industry is outright ripping us off. Games I boughtwith real moneyjust vanish when servers shut down. Take The Crew: Ubisoft announced its shutdown on March31,2024when that happened, even the single-player mode stopped working, and licenses were revoked shortly after. Owning something turned into renting at best . Then theres microtransactions. Loot boxes and gacha systems arent optional funtheyre psychological traps. Research shows theyre structurally and psychologically akin to gambling, especially dangerous for kids and people with gambling issues . In many cases, virtual currencies hide real pricing, randomized rewards hook players in loops, and transparency is almost nonexistent. Children have less impulse control and are disproportionately hit harder by these mechanics . Heres the problem in a nutshell: Bought games vanish when servers are killedpaid games become unplayable. Ownership is a lieno offline options, no backup, just corporate control. Microtransactions mimic gamblingloot boxes, randomized rewards, confusing currencies. Vulnerable users are targetedkids and those prone to gambling face real harm. The message is loud: developers shouldnt be forced to keep servers foreverbut customers deserve access to games they bought. Even simple fixes like offline patches or private server support would make a huge difference . What the EU should demand: Require end-of-life plansoffline modes or tools for private servers. Classify shutting down paid games without compensation as unfair practice. Ban opaque virtual currency systemsprices must be clear in real money. Treat loot boxes and similar mechanics as consumer protection concerns, especially for minorsmandate odds disclosure, spending limits, and enforceable parental controls. Im not against creativity, DLC or optional extras, but buying a game should mean owning it. Kids shouldnt be drawn into gambling loops under the guise of fun. Its really simple: respect players' rights, protect vulnerable users, and stop killing games. Thank you for letting me voice my frustrations at the currect modernisation of the gaming culture, and the online culture in general. From the censorships to the broad predatory systems placed in everything digital today is absurd and needs to be lawfully regulated !
